sections.title: Talking to her before offering yourself as a sacrifice

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Do you wish me to tell you more of Zulrah, human?

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Why do you bother the High Priestess of Zulrah, human?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How do people get chosen as a sacrifice to Zulrah?

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Volunteers offer themselves gladly, knowing that their sacrifice is for the good of the tribe. Also, I grant their families extra rations of food.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: The next sacrifice is a brave gnome who has just come of age. He waits by the boat now.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I won't let you sacrifice him - take me instead!

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: You would offer yourself as a sacrifice in his place? Your dedication to Zulrah must be truly profound to make such an offer...

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: ... and I accept. You shall be our next sacrifice. Board the boat when you are ready, and give glory to Zulrah through your death.

sections.title: Talking to her after offering yourself as a sacrifice but before having encountered Zulrah

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: I see you have not yet offered yourself as a sacrifice to Zulrah, human. Climb into the boat, and the priestess will deliver you to the shrine.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: You offered yourself to Zulrah as a sacrifice, yet here you stand before me. Did Zulrah reject you?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I kinda left the shrine.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Perhaps you should try offering yourself again, before the mighty Zulrah grows impatient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I killed Zulrah.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Very funny, human, but I find that hard to believe. Go and offer yourself as a sacrifice again, before the mighty Zulrah grows impatient.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Have you any advice for surviving against Zulrah?

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: I think you have misunderstood the nature of the sacrifice, human. Never mind, the mighty Zulrah will make the matter clear to you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about Zulrah.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Zulrah is the all-powerful snake god of the southern wastes. He rises from the swamp to show his divine majesty and to accept our homage.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Long ago, our forebears came to this place, not realising that they were trespassing in Zulrah's domain. He arose from the swamp in wrath, and many were slain.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: But Zulrah, in his mercy, allowed some of those transgressors to live, sparing them the punishment they deserved, and allowing them the chance to repent.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Then my mother, oldest and wisest of the tribe, dared to intercede with the mighty Zulrah on behalf of her people, and thus a sacred contract was declared.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Zulrah granted us the right to dwell in his lands, and to eat of their bounty. None shall ever drive us out, for Zulrah protects us.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: All he asks in return is a periodic sacrifice at his sacred shrine. As long as the sacrifices continue, Zulrah will let us live peacefully in his lands.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Willing volunteers are never hard to find, for their sacrifice buys peace for the tribe, and I grant their families extra rations of food.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you all?

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: The southern wastes are a perilous place where travellers often become lost. Elves from the woodland villages, gnomes from the eastern caves, and many others.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Some find their way here, like my forebears did. They eat of the sacred swamp eels that Zulrah sends, and become one with the tribe.

lines.speaker: High Priestess Zul-Harcinqa
lines.text: Their original families and tribes shun them as unclean creatures once they have tasted of the sacred swamp eels, so they remain here to worship Zulrah.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm off.
